description: Same flight with confirmation vs itinerary wording - should match

content_parts.text:
You are comparing calendar events to detect duplicates.

**New Event:**
- Title: Flight to Denver - UA 1234
- Start: 2026-05-10T08:15:00
- End: 2026-05-10T11:45:00
- Location: SFO to DEN
- Description: United Airlines confirmation #ABC123XYZ. Seat 12A.

**Existing Events (same date):**

1. Event ID: event-001
   - Title: Pack for Trip
   - Start: 2026-05-09T20:00:00
   - End: 2026-05-09T21:00:00
   - Location: Home
   - Description: Pack bags for Denver

2. Event ID: event-002
   - Title: UA1234 - San Francisco to Denver
   - Start: 2026-05-10T08:15:00
   - End: 2026-05-10T11:45:00
   - Location: SFO Terminal 3
   - Description: Flight itinerary - departs 8:15am, arrives 11:45am MT

3. Event ID: event-003
   - Title: Hotel Check-in Denver
   - Start: 2026-05-10T15:00:00
   - End: 2026-05-10T15:30:00
   - Location: Hyatt Regency Denver
   - Description: Reservation #456789

**Question:** Is the new event the same as any of the existing events?

Consider events the same if they refer to the same real-world event, even if:
- Wording is slightly different
- One has more details than the other
- Time changed slightly (updates)

Return the matching Event ID (or null if no match) and brief reasoning.

trace.raw_response_text: { "matched_event_id": "event-002", "reasoning": "The new event and event-002 share the same flight number (UA 1234), same departure and arrival times (2026-05-10T08:15:00 to 2026-05-10T11:45:00), and same route (SFO to DEN). The differences in wording (e.g., 'Flight to Denver' vs 'San Francisco to Denver') and location details (general route vs specific terminal) are consistent with referring to the same real-world flight event." }
